text.title: Dormant Lead Reactivation Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules grounding service-business reactivation. Each pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Contacts MUST be segmented into exactly one of {past-client, lost-proposal, nurture-lead}. Mixed-segment sequences are forbidden — each segment gets its own sequence.
rule.rationale: Past clients have prior context that nurture leads lack; bundling them produces tone that fits neither.

rule.statement: A reactivation sequence MUST be ≤3 touches per contact within a quarter. After touch 3, the contact is killed or moved to long-term nurture.
rule.rationale: Beyond 3 touches, additional sends produce more unsubs than replies; small lists punish over-touch.

rule.statement: For contacts in EU / UK / California jurisdictions with consent older than 18 months, touch 1 MUST be a consent re-confirmation (single-question), not an offer.
rule.rationale: Stale consent + offer = unlawful processing in those jurisdictions; re-confirmation is the lesser-harm path.

rule.statement: Every touch MUST reference the specific prior interaction (project name, proposal date, last conversation topic). Generic "hope you're well" openings are rejected.
rule.rationale: Personalized references convert at 3-5× generic openings on small lists.

rule.statement: After each touch + sequence end, the contact's outcome MUST be recorded as one of {engaged, killed, pending}. Pending after touch 3 auto-converts to killed at quarter end.
rule.rationale: Without outcome recording, the same dormants get re-targeted every quarter with diminishing returns.
